old_claim.note: Fiber Optics provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Commercial DWDM at this scope is an optical-fiber transport system that places multiple dense wavelength channels onto fiber links.

source_shape.source_locator: Cisco Introduction to DWDM Technology describes DWDM as transmitting multiple wavelengths over optical fiber to increase capacity.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: DWDM is a fiber-optic transport technology, so optical fiber is part of the scoped technology rather than optional context.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports changing fiber optics from a generic enabler to a required transport medium for this DWDM node.

ontology_before: The graph treated fiber optics as an enabling context.
ontology_after: The graph treats fiber optics as the required transport medium for dense wavelength multiplexing.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to non-fiber free-space wavelength multiplexing.
A future node split made this one represent only abstract multiplexing theory.
short_reason: DWDM optical transport requires optical fiber at this scope.
